title: Framework Performance Audits
concept: Measuring memory footprint and execution overhead introduced by complex orchestrator libraries.

exercises.prompt: What is a danger of over-engineering simple loops in frameworks?

options.text: It can increase resource usage unnecessarily.

exercises.explain: Over-engineering simple loops can lead to increased resource usage, making your framework less efficient.

cards.title: Understanding Performance Audits
cards.body: Performance audits help us measure how efficiently our frameworks use resources. This includes checking memory and execution times.

cards.title: What is Memory Footprint?
cards.body: Memory footprint refers to the amount of memory a program uses while it's running. Smaller footprints are better for efficiency.

cards.title: Orchestrator Libraries Like Gym Equipment
cards.body: Just like some gym equipment can take up more space and require more maintenance without actually being helpful, complex libraries can take up memory and slow down execution.

cards.title: Measuring Memory Footprint
cards.body: If a library uses 200 MB to run a simple task, but a simpler version takes only 50 MB, the latter is clearly more efficient.

cards.title: Myth: More Complex Libraries are Always Better
cards.body: Complex libraries aren't always more efficient; they can add unwanted overhead, decreasing performance.

exercises.prompt: Which of these would be a good reason to audit a framework's performance?

options.text: To check for coding style mistakes.
To measure memory usage and execution speed.
To explain code to new developers.

exercises.explain: Auditing a framework's performance is mainly about understanding memory usage and execution speed for efficiency.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A larger memory footprint is always better for performance.
exercises.explain: A larger memory footprint can actually slow down performance. Smaller footprints are usually better.

exercises.prompt: Performance audits can help us understand the framework's __________.
exercises.sentence: Performance audits can help us understand the framework's memory footprint and execution overhead.
exercises.explain: 'memory footprint' gives insight into how much memory the framework uses.

exercises.prompt: Which option illustrates the need for a performance audit?

options.text: I implemented magic handlers for easier coding.
The application runs but has a high memory usage.
All libraries seem to work without checking performance

exercises.explain: A high memory usage suggests there might be inefficiencies, making a performance audit necessary.

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to check both memory footprint and execution overhead in frameworks?

options.text: To ensure the framework is user-friendly.
To identify if the framework uses too many resources and slows down programs.
To check for bugs and errors in code.
To create more complex libraries and features.

exercises.explain: Checking both helps determine if a framework is efficient and does not waste resources, ensuring better performance.
